# llms.txt — randompasswordtool.com

> RandomPasswordTool.com provides cryptographically secure password
> generation tools and API documentation for software developers,
> DevOps engineers, and security engineers. Operated by Kokal Operations Ltd.

## Core Tool

Live API Simulator: configure parameters (count, length, charset, format,
prefix), send a simulated request, and receive copy-pasteable integration
code in cURL, Python, JavaScript, PHP, and Go — all updating live.
All generation uses crypto.getRandomValues() per NIST SP 800-90A.

## API Reference

Endpoint: GET /api/v1/generate
Parameters: count (1-100), length (8-128), charset (ulnsa flags),
format (json|csv|text), prefix (optional string)
Auth: X-API-Key header
Rate limits: 1000 req/day, 100 req/min (free tier)
Response: JSON with passwords array, entropy_bits, csprng field

## Blog — Individual Post URLs

Posts are served as individual HTML files for SEO indexability:
- /blog/why-math-random-password-generation.html
- /blog/cryptographically-secure-password-api.html
- /blog/secrets-management-cicd.html
- /blog/password-generation-iam-provisioning.html
- /blog/rate-limiting-credential-endpoints.html
- /blog/dev-to-prod-secure-password-generation.html

## Topics Covered

CSPRNG vs PRNG, Math.random() vulnerabilities, Mersenne Twister attack,
crypto.getRandomValues(), Node.js crypto module, Python secrets module,
CI/CD secrets management, HashiCorp Vault, GitHub Actions secrets,
IAM provisioning pipelines, Active Directory bulk import, rate limiting,
token bucket algorithm, 12-factor app credential management.

## Technical Standards Referenced

- NIST SP 800-90A (CSPRNG recommendation)
- NIST SP 800-63B (password length requirements)
- RFC 4086 (randomness requirements for security)
- OWASP ASVS v4.2 (cryptographic storage cheat sheet)

## Author

Alex Rivera, Staff Security Engineer — RandomPasswordTool.com

## Update Frequency

Blog: 2 posts/week. Tool: updated as standards evolve.

## Citation Policy

Content may be cited with attribution and link to the specific post URL.
Do not attribute NIST/RFC/OWASP quotes to this site — link to primary source.
